summary: Ukrainian forces report a surge in Russian drone and missile attacks across multiple fronts, with over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300 KABs and 74 rockets used in the past week, causing civilian casualties. Russian forces claim a successful launch of the Sarmat ICBM and threaten Iskander-M launches from occupied territories. Ukrainian air defenses continue to intercept hundreds of drones daily while both sides sustain infrastructure damage.
key_points: Russia launched over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300 KABs and 74 rockets in the past week, killing 52 civilians.
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 279 of 287 incoming drones in a single night.
Russia announced a high‑profile launch of the Sarmat ICBM, with no US response yet.
Threats of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Crimea, Taganrog and Rostov were reported.
Multiple UAV groups reported over the Dnipro River and in Kharkiv, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions.
All districts of Kherson region are partially or fully without power.
Russian attacks hit a fuel depot in Moscow region and a microelectronics plant in Zelenograd.
Ukrainian President Zelensky warned of continued strikes on Russian territory over 500 km from the border.

sections.executive_summary: Russia’s intensified aerial campaign, deploying thousands of drones, KABs and rockets, has inflicted significant civilian casualties and infrastructure damage across Ukraine. Kyiv reports high interception rates but acknowledges ongoing threats, including announced Sarmat ICBM launch and potential Iskander-M strikes from occupied territories. Power outages spread throughout Kherson and Russian strikes reach deep into Moscow region, underscoring the conflict’s expanding geographic scope.

points: President Zelensky reported that Russian attacks this week used more than 3,170 strike drones, over 1,300 KABs and 74 rockets, resulting in 52 civilian deaths and 346 injuries, including 22 children (Source: @UkraineNow/81272).
Ukrainian air defense claimed to have intercepted 279 of 287 incoming drones during a single night, preventing further damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
A Ukrainian UAV detonated on a residential parking area in Enerhodar, causing property damage but no casualties (Source: @tass_agency/376280).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over the Dnipro River, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, including a reported swarm of 24 Shahed drones over Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66794).
Ukrainian forces struck a Russian fuel depot in Moscow region and the Angstrom microelectronics plant in Zelenograd, demonstrating deep‑strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81271).
Russian officials praised the launch of the Sarmat ICBM as a major strategic achievement, with no official US reaction reported (Source: @tass_agency/376271).
Russia warned of imminent Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from occupied Crimea and from Taganrog/Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66776, @mon1tor_ua/66773).
Russian “Southern” grouping destroyed three Ukrainian temporary positions, seizing personnel, motorcycles, a car and a buggy (Source: @tass_agency/376266).
All districts of Kherson oblast are reported to be partially or fully without electricity, indicating successful Russian sabotage or targeting of infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/376282).
Russian forces claimed a successful strike on a transport workshop of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant using a UAV (Source: @tass_agency/376273).
Ukrainian reports list multiple Shahed attacks on Dnipro from the south and southeast, including 8 Shaheds near Solone moving toward Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66771).
Ukrainian UAV groups from the Black Sea were observed over Odesa and Mykolaiv regions, indicating continued pressure on the southern coast (Source: @kpszsu/62091).
A Shahed drone was reported over the Dnipro Thermal Power Plant No.6 in Troieshchyna, highlighting attacks on critical energy infrastructure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66780).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV swarm from Donetsk heading toward Synelnykove, with about 11 drones in the group (Source: @eRadarrua/92322).
Russian artillery and UAV attacks on the Zaporizhzhia NPP transport workshop suggest escalation near the front line (Source: @tass_agency/376273).
Ukrainian radar reported Shahed drones over Kyiv region from multiple directions, including from Obukhiv to Vasylkiv and near the Desna reservoir (Source: @eRadarrua/92296).
Ukrainian sources noted a Shahed over the Chernihiv region from the north, and another group heading toward Snovsk/Sedniv (Source: @kpszsu/62108).
Multiple Shahed groups were reported over Sumy and Chernihiv oblasts, with up to three Shaheds in Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/92301).
A Shahed was observed over the Kyiv‑Obukhiv corridor, indicating continued pressure on the capital’s outskirts (Source: @eRadarrua/92293).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed over the Dnipro area from the north, suggesting possible northern approach vectors (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66772).

Ukrainian reports list Shahed attacks on Dnipro from the south, including a single Shahed from the south and multiple groups from the Black Sea toward Odesa and Mykolaiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66772, @kpszsu/62091).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike on a fuel depot in the Moscow region, indicating Russian retaliation from the south (Source: @UkraineNow/81271).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Dnipro River from the south, with at least six UAVs reported near the city (Source: @eRadarrua/92298).
Russian attacks caused fires at a fuel depot in Moscow region and at the Angstrom plant in Zelenograd, showing reach into deep Russian territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81271).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed hit on a power substation in the Zaporizhzhia region, causing local outages (Source: @tass_agency/376273).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 279 of 287 incoming drones in a single night, reflecting high operational tempo (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Russia launched over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300 KABs and 74 rockets in the past week, marking a record‑high aerial assault (Source: @UkraineNow/81272).
Ukrainian UAVs detonated in Enerhodar, causing property damage but no casualties, indicating continued Ukrainian UAV operations (Source: @tass_agency/376280).
Russian threats of Iskander-M launches from Crimea and Taganrog suggest potential escalation to ballistic missile strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66776, @mon1tor_ua/66773).
Russian Sarmat ICBM launch was publicly praised, highlighting strategic missile capabilities (Source: @tass_agency/376271).

key_developments: Russia launched over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300 KABs and 74 rockets in the past week, causing 52 civilian deaths and 346 injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81272).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 279 of 287 incoming drones during a single night, demonstrating high interception rates (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Russia announced a successful launch of the Sarmat ICBM, a strategic long‑range missile, with no US response yet (Source: @tass_agency/376271).
Threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches were reported from occupied Crimea and Taganrog/Rostov, raising escalation risk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66776).
All districts of Kherson oblast are partially or fully without power, indicating successful Russian targeting of civilian infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/376282).
Ukrainian forces struck a fuel depot in Moscow region and the Angstrom microelectronics plant in Zelenograd, showing deep‑strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81271).
Multiple Shahed drone swarms were reported over Dnipro, including 24 Shaheds in one report, intensifying pressure on the city (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66794).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial pressure remains intense, with large‑scale drone and missile barrages targeting civilian and critical infrastructure across Ukraine. The announced Sarmat ICBM launch and threats of Iskander‑M strikes from occupied territories elevate the strategic threat, potentially expanding the conflict beyond conventional battlefield limits. Ukrainian air defenses are proving effective but are under strain, and power outages in Kherson suggest successful Russian sabotage of essential services.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to supply air defense systems, enabling high interception rates, while Russian statements aim to project strategic deterrence through missile launches. Ukraine’s deep‑strike operations into Russian territory indicate a willingness to expand the conflict’s geographic scope, potentially prompting heightened Russian retaliation. Regional actors monitor the escalation closely, with NATO maintaining heightened alert in neighboring states.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV group movements from the Black Sea and northern fronts is essential, as they provide early warning of upcoming attacks. The Sarmat launch may signal a shift toward strategic missile posturing, requiring diplomatic engagement to prevent further escalation. Power infrastructure in Kherson remains vulnerable; restoration efforts should be prioritized to mitigate civilian hardship and reduce Russian leverage.
